key_insight: The paper estimates branch material parameters from observed deformation so a finite-element branch model can guide safer, deformation-aware manipulation.
engineering_relevance: This enables agricultural robots to reposition or stabilize branches while reducing unwanted bending and improving visibility in dense foliage.
method: Build a tetrahedral branch model from point-cloud geometry.
Simulate branch mechanics with the finite element method.
Iteratively fit material parameters using real deformation observations, then plan a path that minimizes deformation energy.
evidence_signal: Across 30 trials on branches with varied geometry and material properties, the method reduced deformation energy by 35.69% while increasing path length by 8.10% on average.
watch_item: Performance depends on accurate point-cloud reconstruction and observed deformation data, so noisy sensing or limited branch contact information could degrade parameter estimation and planning.
paper_title: Modeling Branches for Active Manipulation using Iterative Parameter Estimation
